task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window (likely in ImageJ) displaying the 'Fluorescent Cells' image, which originally features distinct red and green cellular structures.

task_completion.description: Map the original Red channel to Magenta
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image or a specific channel window shows the previously red cellular structures now appearing magenta (purple-pink). This might be seen via LUT (Lookup Table) adjustments or channel merging.

task_completion.description: Keep the Green and Blue channels unchanged
task_completion.visual_evidence: The green cellular structures in the image remain green and unchanged in their spatial distribution alongside the newly magenta structures.

task_completion.description: Convert the image to a flattened RGB composite
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window title or status bar indicates the image type is 'RGB' (e.g., 'RGB' text in the window header), meaning it is no longer a multi-channel composite with separate active sliders.
A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'accessible_composite.tif' being saved in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory, or a terminal/file manager verifying the file's creation at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's GUI to process the image rather than relying solely on terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI menus (e.g., Image > Color > Merge Channels, or LUT menus) in an application like ImageJ.

integrity.description: The agent actively manipulated the image channels rather than downloading or copying a pre-made solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps are visible, such as split channel windows, color assignment dialogs, or the 'Merge Channels' tool being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file to the genuinely requested directory without spoofing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog or file explorer explicitly shows the path /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The ground truth 'Fluorescent Cells' image is a 512x512 JPEG with distinct Red and Green channels and minimal Blue content. The expected output must be saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_composite.tif. The final image should visually show the original red structures as magenta, while the green structures remain unchanged.
